answer:
Walter Lippmann was an American journalist, political commentator, and author. He is best known for his work on public opinion and the media. H. P. Lovecraft was an American author known for his horror fiction, particularly his works featuring the Cthulhu Mythos. While both were influential in their respective fields, they were not both authors in the same sense. Lippmann was primarily a journalist and political commentator, while Lovecraft was a novelist and short story writer. Therefore, it is not accurate to say that both were authors in the same capacity.

<answer> No </answer>
